changes_made: Renamed k8s_secret_masking_enabled to k8s_secret_masking_implemented for clarity
Renamed k8s_secret_hashing_enabled to k8s_secret_hashing_implemented for clarity
review_notes: The functions map well to the NIST SI-19(4) control by focusing on de-identification techniques such as encryption, masking, and hashing. The original function names for masking and hashing were slightly ambiguous, as 'enabled' could imply a binary state rather than an action or implementation. Adjustments were made to better reflect the implementation aspect of these controls. The functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes security practices.
naming_improvements: Use 'implemented' instead of 'enabled' for masking and hashing to better convey the action being taken
title: De-Identification - De - identification | removal , masking , encryption , hashing , or replacement
step1_reasoning: The control SI-19(4) focuses on de-identification through removal, masking, encryption, hashing, or replacement. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that sensitive data stored as Secrets is encrypted at rest using etcd encryption. Additionally, masking and hashing can be implemented by ensuring that sensitive data is appropriately transformed before being stored in Secrets. These practices align with the control's requirement for data protection and de-identification.
